energy_logic_rationale: Office daytime load (9-17h) overlaps with PV generation 60-80%. Battery covers evening cleaning/server load. Best for PV ratio > 15 kWp per 1000 m².
risk_or_caveat: Requires PV. Empty weekends → idle capacity unless TOU added.

energy_logic_rationale: German Leistungspreis or NL transport tariff is 50% of office bill. Predictable 11:00-14:00 peak → battery discharge to clip top 15-25%.
risk_or_caveat: Demand charge must exist (DE/NL/BE/UK/IT). Pure energy markets (NO/SE) yield less.

energy_logic_rationale: Charge off-peak (€0.06) → discharge peak (€0.28) = ~€0.18/kWh spread minus 6% RTE loss. Office matches EPEX day-night pattern.
risk_or_caveat: Spread must be >€0.12/kWh post-tax. Erodes in flat-tariff markets.

energy_logic_rationale: Office can survive 1-2h outages (UPS sufficient). Backup as primary = wasted capacity. Only justified for trading floor or data room.
risk_or_caveat: Low energy-domain value unless mission-critical IT.

energy_logic_rationale: Office load steady → battery free during day. FCR weekly tender DE/DK = €4-8/kW/week. Symmetric product safest.
risk_or_caveat: Requires aggregator (€0.5-1.5k/yr fees). Min 1 MW pool → small office unfit.

energy_logic_rationale: UK T-4 auction €19-35/kW/year. Lower revenue but firm 4-yr contract.
risk_or_caveat: UK/IT only. Min derated capacity 1 MW.

energy_logic_rationale: If office EV fleet ≥ 10 cars → V2G aggregator (NL/DE Pilot). 5-10 kWh export per car/day off-peak hours.
risk_or_caveat: Bidirectional charger CAPEX. Driver behavior risk. Best at >20 cars.

energy_logic_rationale: Retail 9-21h matches PV until 17h. AC + lighting heavy. PV+battery cuts grid 30-45%.
risk_or_caveat: Mall/big-box retail only — high-street rents prevent rooftop PV.

energy_logic_rationale: AC + checkout/POS spikes 12:00-15:00 → demand peak. Battery clips peak in cooling season.
Retail closes 21:00 → 9h off-peak window. Charge fully, discharge during 17:00-20:00 evening peak.
Refrigeration in food retail mandates 4-8h backup. Cold-chain loss €5-15k per outage event.
risk_or_caveat: Food retail only. Non-food retail waste capacity.

energy_logic_rationale: Retail load predictable → some FCR window. Lower than office due to evening discharge need.
risk_or_caveat: Small retail unfit. Big-box chain pool model required.

energy_logic_rationale: Lower stack rank.
Retail customers don't park long enough. EV charging revenue (B2C) better than V2G.
24/7 base load + PV overlap on rooftop. Hotel pool/spa heating ideal battery sink in evening.
risk_or_caveat: Pool/spa heating must be electric, not gas.

energy_logic_rationale: Less defined peak (continuous load) → lower benefit vs office.
risk_or_caveat: Best for >150-room hotels with HVAC central plant.

energy_logic_rationale: Hotel uses electricity 24/7 — TOU spread captured continuously. EV charging stations boost case.
Guest safety + reputation = mandatory backup. ASHRAE 90.1 + many EU codes require >4h emergency power. Battery superior to diesel for indoor air quality.
risk_or_caveat: Sizing must cover egress lighting + 1 elevator + critical IT — typically 60-100 kWh per 100 rooms.

energy_logic_rationale: 24/7 demand competes with FCR symmetric obligation. Limited window.
risk_or_caveat: Best as secondary stream when primary strategy is backup.

energy_logic_rationale: Lower stack rank.
Guest EV charging stations + V2G aggregator → off-peak hours when occupancy low. Mature in NL.
risk_or_caveat: Park duration variable — guests typically 10h overnight = perfect V2G window.

energy_logic_rationale: 24/7 base + PV overlap moderate. Some PV → battery to cover surgical block evening load.
risk_or_caveat: Surgical block can never depend on solo PV+battery — backup must remain.

energy_logic_rationale: Imaging (MRI/CT) spike loads 10:00-16:00. Battery clips imaging-induced demand peak.
risk_or_caveat: Imaging schedule unpredictable — DR (Demand Response) algorithm needed.

energy_logic_rationale: Continuous load reduces arbitrage window — partial benefit only.
EN 50171 / DIN VDE 0108 mandates emergency power for life-safety circuits. Diesel still legal but battery+PV hybrid increasingly required for ESG.
risk_or_caveat: Sizing: typically 200-800 kWh per hospital wing. UPS-grade response time (<10 ms) requires high-power chemistry (LFP or Hybrid+Supercap).

energy_logic_rationale: Mission-critical load makes FCR symmetric obligation risky.
Lower stack rank.
Ambulance/staff fleet → cannot risk discharged batteries.
School day 8-17h overlaps PV. Holiday gaps (summer) → battery soaks excess for grid export.
risk_or_caveat: Summer holidays = lowest load when PV peaks → excess export.

energy_logic_rationale: University labs spike load. Most schools no demand charge.
Off-peak nights + weekends = long charging window. Schools closed 17:00-08:00.
Egress lighting mandate + IT continuity → modest backup need.
Predictable schedule fits FCR week-tender.
Lower stack rank.
Staff parking lots 8h/day → V2G window. Student fleet too transient.
Warehouse roof area massive → high PV capacity (>500 kWp typical). Battery captures excess for forklift fleet evening charge.
risk_or_caveat: Forklift fleet must be electric (Li-Ion or Pb), not propane.

energy_logic_rationale: Forklift fast-charging spikes load 10-50x base. Cross-docking 6-22h with morning/evening surges. Demand charges in DE/NL/BE/UK punish unmanaged peaks.
risk_or_caveat: Critical — without peak-shaving, demand charges exceed energy cost.

energy_logic_rationale: Night operations + early-morning shifts use off-peak rates. Some logistics 24/7 → arbitrage continuous.
Cold chain (frozen/refrigerated warehouse) needs continuity. Otherwise modest.
Large connection size + flexible discharge → ideal FCR pool member.
UK fulfilment centers + DRSP eligible for T-1 auctions.
Truck depots (overnight parked) + electric truck fleets (DAF/Volvo eTrucks 2025+) → biggest V2G volumes coming 2026-2030.
risk_or_caveat: V2G regulation for heavy-duty still emerging.

energy_logic_rationale: Roof too small relative to load. PV trivial vs 1-10 MW data center demand.
Cooling-system + IT load peaks during hot afternoons. Battery + free-cooling integration cuts demand 10-18%.
risk_or_caveat: Cooling-correlated peak well-known. Battery sizing critical.

energy_logic_rationale: 24/7 load = lower arbitrage benefit. Partial only.
Uptime SLA (99.999%) requires UPS-grade battery + generator. Modern DC moving from VRLA to LFP for footprint + lifecycle.
Major US/EU hyperscalers monetizing UPS pools (DC FCR a.k.a. 'Dynamic Containment EFR'). National Grid UK pioneer.
risk_or_caveat: Customer SLA risk — must contract carefully.

energy_logic_rationale: UK Capacity Market well-suited (Aviva/Microsoft DC participants 2023-).
Data centers do not have meaningful EV fleets.
